Nothing drives me nuts faster. Nothing will make me go berserk faster than watching a salesperson. Take a call without a notepad, fresh nice clean notepad. I'm not talking about when he's sticky notes. What are you doing man? You don't value the information you're getting. You guys are jamming all your content in on one page or you're not putting your information into a CRM. You don't value the questions you're getting answered and if you don't value data information then you probably don't value your customers and you're probably not making the kind of money you should be making. Always have a notepad. If you can't type fast, if you're not on a headset forget it for a computer. You can't do it. It's impossible. Okay? So if you're on a phone or if you're on a speaker phone or if you're on a headset or you've got a dollar, okay? You're on a cell phone. You could be in all three of those situations, right? I need a notepad. Something I can scribble on. One sheet or two sheets or three sheets. I don't care how much paper you waste. Quit worrying about wasting paper because you're wasting customers and opportunities, okay? I walk into my call center and I jump on calls. I'm listening to salespeople. I look at the, I'm like where's the data dude? Where's the guy's name? How many employees does he have? That's what we do. We help companies increase their sales. I don't see anything. I see somebody writing, they got notes for the last week on one sheet of paper. I'm like you don't value my customers because clearly they're not your customers because you're not going to make them a customer because you don't have any information. You guys are doing research. You're doing research before the call and then you don't do research during the call. You don't collect the data. Why do you ask a question? Why do you ask the customer a question if you're not going to write down the answer? And more importantly, you need to write down what they're saying to you and exactly how they're saying it to you and when they talk to you put it in quotes so that when, and if you don't follow this call, you need to follow them up. You can talk about what they said, how they said it, the words they use, the chances of you remembering everything a prospect says on the call two days from now when your manager makes a follow call or somebody else wants to help you or so like they're so small, it's unbelievable. I'm telling you this because I made this mistake. I've made it so many thousands of times. I thought, okay, I'm going to remember this one caller. I remember the problem is I do remember it and then I confused it with the other hundred calls I made that day and the three weeks that went on and all the other things that happened in your life. If you ask the right questions and demand answers and really control a call with a script that you've role played, you will get every piece of data you need to know how to sell somebody and how to close them. Here are a couple of things you need to know about your notes. Number one, write them down. Write everything down. Okay, everything down. That's meaningful. Write down as much possible in the call. I don't need to sit there and write everything he says. I need to write down key questions from my script.
